{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779","id":2519742842,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6WMDl6","number":4779,"title":"Problem mixing lfortran and clang++ compilation","user":{"login":"mottelet","id":8794797,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3OTQ3OTc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8794797?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet","html_url":"https://github.com/mottelet","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-09-11T13:23:07Z","updated_at":"2024-09-11T13:53:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello,

I have a problem trying to test a simplified calling sequence C++ -> Fortran -> C++ testing exception reporting through a a Fortran subroutine (it work e.g. when using the g++/gfortran pair) with clang++ and lfortran. Tests have been done on a M1 mac with latest miniforge-conda and clang, clang++ from cxx-compiler package and lfortran package.

The fortran subroutine in `testf.f` source file is:
```
      subroutine testf(simul)
      external simul
      call simul(0)
      end
```
and the c++ part (main and function) is in `main.cpp` source file:
```
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

typedef void (*fun_t)(int *ind);
extern "C" void testf_(fun_t);
void cppsimul(int *i);

int main() {
    cout << "in main\n";
    try
    {
        testf_(cppsimul);        
    }
    catch (int e)
    {
        cout << "Caught exception\n";
    }
  return 0;
}

void cppsimul(int *i)
{
    cout << "in cppsimul\n";
    throw 0;
}
```
Compilation is done with:
```
lfortran -c --implicit-interface  testf.f 
clang++ main.cpp testf.o -o main
```
and the first step is OK and yields `testf.o` but for the c++ compilation and link i get
```
% clang++ main.cpp testf.o -o main         
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  "_testf(void (*)(int*))", referenced from:
      _main in main-73c7ba.o
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
clang++: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
```
Which flags are needed for the lfortran call ?

In fact, when I look into the `testf.o` object file, I get
```
 % objdump -t testf.o                      

testf.o:	file format mach-o arm64

SYMBOL TABLE:
0000000000000000 l     F __TEXT,__text ltmp0
```
which shows that no symbol is exported.

Thanks for help","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2343743394","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779#issuecomment-2343743394","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4779","id":2343743394,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Lsq-i","user":{"login":"mottelet","id":8794797,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3OTQ3OTc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8794797?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet","html_url":"https://github.com/mottelet","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-11T13:53:17Z","updated_at":"2024-09-11T13:53:17Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> ```
> in main
> in cppsimul
> Caught exception
> ```

Thanks ! And I see that the exception is correctly propagated, the couple clang/lfortran does it right !

```
c
c-----------------------------------------------------------------------
c
c     Count the number of elements equal to a specified integer value.
c
      integer function icnteq (n, array, value)
c
      integer    n, value
      integer    array(*)
c
      k = 0
      do 10 i = 1, n
         if (array(i) .eq. value) k = k + 1
   10 continue
      icnteq = k
c
      return
      end
```
I get
```
% lfortran --std legacy icnteq.f
zsh: segmentation fault  lfortran --std legacy icnteq.f
```
and adding `--verbose` yields
```
(base) mottelet@gpport29-eth-cr UTIL % lfortran --verbose --std legacy icnteq.f
ASR Pass starts: 'nested_vars'
ASR Pass ends: 'nested_vars'
ASR Pass starts: 'global_stmts'
ASR Pass ends: 'global_stmts'
ASR Pass starts: 'transform_optional_argument_functions'

      integer function icnteq (n, array, value)

```
c
c-----------------------------------------------------------------------
c
c     Count the number of elements equal to a specified integer value.
c
      integer function icnteq (n, array, value)
c
      integer    n, value
      integer    array(*)
c
      k = 0
      do 10 i = 1, n
         if (array(i) .eq. value) k = k + 1
   10 continue
      icnteq = k
c
      return
      end
```

```
% lfortran --std legacy icnteq.f
zsh: segmentation fault  lfortran --std legacy icnteq.f

Now it works on the test Arpack file:
```
(base) mottelet@gpport29-eth-cr UTIL % /Users/mottelet/git/lfortran/inst/bin/lfortran -c --std legacy --show-llvm icnteq.f
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

%array = type { ptr, i32, ptr, i1, i32 }
%dimension_descriptor = type { i32, i32, i32 }

define i32 @icnteq(ptr %n, ptr %array, ptr %value) {
.entry:
  %i = alloca i32, align 4
  %icnteq = alloca i32, align 4
  %k = alloca i32, align 4
  store i32 0, ptr %k, align 4
  store i32 0, ptr %i, align 4
  br label %loop.head

loop.head:                                        ; preds = %goto_target, %.entry
  %0 = load i32, ptr %i, align 4
  %1 = add i32 %0, 1
  %2 = load i32, ptr %n, align 4
  %3 = icmp sle i32 %1, %2
  br i1 %3, label %loop.body, label %loop.end

loop.body:                                        ; preds = %loop.head
  %4 = load i32, ptr %i, align 4
  %5 = add i32 %4, 1
  store i32 %5, ptr %i, align 4
  %6 = load i32, ptr %i, align 4
  %7 = getelementptr %array, ptr %array, i32 0, i32 2
  %8 = load ptr, ptr %7, align 8
  %9 = getelementptr inbounds %dimension_descriptor, ptr %8, i32 0
  %10 = getelementptr %dimension_descriptor, ptr %9, i32 0, i32 1
  %11 = load i32, ptr %10, align 4
  %12 = sub i32 %6, %11
  %13 = getelementptr %dimension_descriptor, ptr %9, i32 0, i32 0
  %14 = load i32, ptr %13, align 4
  %15 = mul i32 %14, %12
  %16 = add i32 0, %15
  %17 = getelementptr %array, ptr %array, i32 0, i32 1
  %18 = load i32, ptr %17, align 4
  %19 = add i32 %16, %18
  %20 = getelementptr %array, ptr %array, i32 0, i32 0
  %21 = load ptr, ptr %20, align 8
  %22 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %21, i32 %19
  %23 = load i32, ptr %22, align 4
  %24 = load i32, ptr %value, align 4
  %25 = icmp eq i32 %23, %24
  br i1 %25, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %loop.body
  %26 = load i32, ptr %k, align 4
  %27 = add i32 %26, 1
  store i32 %27, ptr %k, align 4
  br label %ifcont

else:                                             ; preds = %loop.body
  br label %ifcont

ifcont:                                           ; preds = %else, %then
  br label %goto_target

goto_target:                                      ; preds = %ifcont
  br label %loop.head

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.head
  %28 = load i32, ptr %k, align 4
  store i32 %28, ptr %icnteq, align 4
  br label %return

unreachable_after_return:                         ; No predecessors!
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %unreachable_after_return, %loop.end
  %29 = load i32, ptr %icnteq, align 4
  ret i32 %29
}

```
curl -LO https://github.com/opencollab/arpack-ng/archive/refs/tags/3.9.1.tar.gz
tar xvzf 3.9.1.tar.gz
cd arpack-ng-3.9.1
mkdir build
cd build
cmake -DCMAKE_Fortran_COMPILER=/Users/mottelet/git/lfortran/inst/bin/lfortran -DBLAS_LIBRARIES="-framework Accelerate" -DLAPACK_LIBRARIES="-framework Accelerate" -DCMAKE_Fortran_FLAGS="--std legacy" ..
make -j
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2361293473/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

### What happened?

Some macOS arm64 dynamic libraries have a blank sdk number i.e. "sdk n/a" which break the notarizing process of applications build using conda environment, unless the library validation process is disabled, which decreases the security level (and makes the notarizing process almost useless). For example here is part of the output of 
```
otool -l $CONDA_PREFIX/lib/libgfortran.dylib
```
the sdk number is n/a:
```
Load command 9
      cmd LC_BUILD_VERSION
  cmdsize 32
 platform 1
    minos 11.0
      sdk n/a
   ntools 1
     tool 3
  version 609.0
```
Other libraries have the same problem, e.g.
```
libgettextlib.dylib
libgfortran.dylib
libgirepository-2.0
libquadmath.dylib
librbio.dylib
libreadline.8.2.dylib
```

Checklist

- [X] I added a descriptive title
- [X] I searched open reports and couldn't find a duplicate

### What happened?

Some macOS arm64 dynamic libraries have a blank sdk number i.e. "sdk n/a" which break the notarizing process of applications build using conda environment, unless the library validation process is disabled, which decreases the security level (and makes the notarizing process almost useless). For example here is part of the output of an arm64 library
```
otool -l $CONDA_PREFIX/lib/libgfortran.dylib
```
the sdk number is n/a:
```
Load command 9
      cmd LC_BUILD_VERSION
  cmdsize 32
 platform 1
    minos 11.0
      sdk n/a
   ntools 1
     tool 3
  version 609.0
```
Other libraries have the same problem, e.g. arm64 lbraries
```
libgettextlib.dylib
libgfortran.dylib
libgirepository-2.0
libquadmath.dylib
librbio.dylib
libreadline.8.2.dylib
```
and x86_64 libraries
```
libamd.2.dylib
libarpack.2.dylib
libblas.3.dylib
libbtf.1.dylib
libbz2.dylib
libc++.1.dylib
libcamd.2.dylib
libccolamd.2.dylib
libcholmod.3.dylib
libcolamd.2.dylib
libfftw3.3.dylib
libfftw3.dylib
libgcc_s.1.1.dylib
libgfortran.5.dylib
libhdf5.103.dylib
libhdf5_hl.100.dylib
libklu.1.dylib
liblz4.1.dylib
liblzma.5.dylib
liblzo2.2.dylib
libomp.dylib
libopenblas.0.dylib
libpcre.1.dylib
libpcreposix.0.dylib
libquadmath.0.dylib:
libsuitesparseconfig.5.dylib:
```
but this list is not exhaustive and depends on the architecture (arn64 or x86_64).

Comment:

Hello,

I installed conda 23.11.0 on macOS x86_64 and all libraries were build against 10.9 sdk, which is fine as usual, but I saw that  current libcxx-16.0.6 has

Load command 8
      cmd LC_VERSION_MIN_MACOSX
  cmdsize 16
  version 10.9
      sdk 10.14

hence no binaries are able to run on old macs having macOS < 10.14 because, for example, std::align_val_t is only available on macOS 10.13 and newer.

Why libcxx was not built against 10.9 SDK as other packages ?

S.

","user":{"login":"mottelet","id":8794797,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3OTQ3OTc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8794797?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet","html_url":"https://github.com/mottelet","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mottelet/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":527058030,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1MjcwNTgwMzA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libcxx-feedstock/labels/question","name":"question","color":"cc317c","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-04-29T16:31:10Z","updated_at":"2024-04-30T07:23:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Comment:

Hello,

I installed conda 23.11.0 on macOS x86_64 and all libraries were build against 10.9 sdk, which is fine as usual, but I saw that  current libcxx-16.0.6 has

Load command 8
      cmd LC_VERSION_MIN_MACOSX
  cmdsize 16
  version 10.9
      **sdk 10.14**

hence no binaries are able to run on old macs having macOS < 10.14 because, for example, std::align_val_t is only available on macOS 10.13 and newer. I have the following issue when running on a macOS 10.9 machine an application compiled against this libc++:
```
dyld: Symbol not found: __ZdaPvSt11align_val_t
  Referenced from: /Applications/scilab-macos_fmemopen.app/Contents/bin/../lib/thirdparty/libc++.1.dylib
  Expected in: /usr/lib/libc++abi.dylib
 in /Applications/scilab-macos_fmemopen.app/Contents/bin/../lib/thirdparty/libc++.1.dylib
```

Why libcxx was not built against 10.9 SDK as other packages ?

I see here : https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/1844#issuecomment-1467234057 
that libcxx 17 has recently [dropped](https://reviews.llvm.org/D145012) support for macOS<10.13 when building the shared libcxx library (I noted this in https://github.com/conda-forge/libcxx-feedstock/issues/110 when the RFC opened).

but it seems to have been already the same for libcxx 16. 

setting min macOS version to 10.9 is useless if build is made with newer 10.14 SDK (which masks unavailable features in 10.9)

S.

Just a remark about OpenMP in Sundials and its potential interest. It is not true that OpenMP can speed up only nonlinear solver part. For very large (typically a million dofs) and stiff (even purely linear) ode, when **iterative** linear solvers are the only way to go (and no matrix of any kind are used), then using NVector_OpenMP allows substantial performance gains. Profiling shows that in that typical case,  2/3 of the cpu time is elapsed in N_VLinearSum, N_VDotProd, N_VLinearSum, N_VWrmsNorm, N_VScale   and 1/3 elapsed in the RHS computation (that can be also crunched with OpenMP). My two cents...","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/Sundials.jl/issues/comments/1269544450/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
